era_context: Old Babylonian period in Mesopotamia; Second Intermediate Period in Egypt; Indus Valley Civilization mature phase. This was an era of regional state formation, with Babylon, Egypt, and the Indus Valley Civilization as the major literate civilizations. The Mediterranean world was in the Early Bronze Age; the Aegean had not yet developed the palace civilizations of the Middle Bronze Age.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. (No events documented for this year.)
historiographic_note: 1904 BCE is not documented in any known historical source. The year is archaeologically indistinguishable from surrounding years. Historical knowledge of this period derives from broader chronological frameworks, king lists, and stratigraphic sequences rather than year-specific records. The absence of documentation is expected for this period and region.
